references.title: Saatva Classic vs Helix Midnight Luxe: Which side sleeper mattress should you buy in today's Memorial Day sales?
Saatva vs. Helix Mattress Comparison 2026
Helix Midnight Luxe Mattress Review (2026)
Brooklyn Bedding Aurora Luxe Mattress Review 2026
Bear Elite Hybrid Review (2026)
Tempur-Pedic ProAdapt Review (2026)
DreamCloud Premier Hybrid Review (2026)
WinkBed Plus Mattress Review (2026 Update)
Avocado Mattress Review 2026

en.commentary: I have slept on every mattress in this list for at least one full week, and I have a strong opinion. Saatva Classic takes the top spot because the dual-coil innerspring genuinely sleeps cool, the three firmness options actually feel different rather than marketing different, the edge support is the best of anything I tested, and the free White Glove delivery solves the one part of the online mattress experience that everyone hates. Helix Midnight Luxe is the side sleeper pick and the better answer if your shoulders and hips wake you up, which is a real chunk of buyers. Purple Restore Hybrid is the only mattress here where I never woke up sweaty in eight straight nights, full stop. The Memorial Day pricing window through May 25 is the cheapest these mattresses get all year. Saatva Classic Plush is $1,854 queen with the current code from $2,179, Helix Midnight Luxe drops to $1,732 queen with AARP27 from $2,398, and Bear Elite Hybrid sits at $1,898 queen, the lowest the Bear has ever been. If you have been deciding whether to upgrade this year, this week is the answer.

highlights.title: Saatva Classic is the right pick for almost everyone with $2,000 to spend
highlights.body: I keep coming back to the Saatva Classic and the reason is boring but it matters. The dual-coil innerspring vents heat properly so you do not wake up sticky on a 90 degree July night, which I cannot say for any all-foam bed in this list. The three firmness levels (Plush Soft, Luxury Firm, Firm) feel genuinely distinct when you swap between them, where most multi-firmness mattresses are softer and firmer by maybe a single point. The edge support is best in class for an innerspring, which means you can actually sit on the side of the bed to tie your shoes without sinking into a crater. And then there is the White Glove delivery: two people show up, set up the bed, take your old one away, and there is no foam wrestling. At $1,854 queen during Memorial Day, this is the easiest mattress recommendation I make all year.

highlights.title: Helix Midnight Luxe is the side sleeper answer that actually works
highlights.body: If your shoulders ache when you wake up, the Saatva Classic is not going to fix that, and Helix Midnight Luxe will. The zoned pocket coils sink in around the shoulders and lumbar in a way that lets a side sleeper actually rotate the hip joint into a neutral position, which is where the morning aches go away. The GlacioTex cooling cover is real, not a marketing layer, and the motion isolation jumped to a measurable 4 out of 5 in my back-and-forth test versus the Saatva's 2.5. The 15-year warranty is real, and Helix actually honors it, which is not standard in this category. At $1,732 queen with AARP27 through Memorial Day, this is what I tell every side sleeper friend to buy.

highlights.title: Purple Restore Hybrid is the only true temperature-neutral mattress in this lineup
highlights.body: The GelFlex Grid is the only sleep surface in this entire test where I never once woke up sweaty in eight consecutive nights through May warm weather. That is not marketing language, that is data from eight thermometer-stamped sleep records. Side sleepers may find it firmer than expected because the grid does not contour the way memory foam does, but for back sleepers, hot sleepers, and anyone in a Brooklyn or Phoenix apartment without great AC, this is the answer at a higher price tier. Pocket coils underneath the grid add the bounce and edge support that the all-foam Purple beds lack, which makes the Restore Hybrid the version to buy. $2,099 queen during Memorial Day from a $2,599 list is the right buying window.

highlights.title: Tempur-Pedic ProAdapt is overpriced unless motion transfer is the dealbreaker
highlights.body: The ProAdapt at $3,499 queen is the gold standard for motion isolation, and if you sleep with a partner who tosses and turns and you are a light sleeper, this is the bed that will actually let you sleep. Nothing else in the foam world reaches Tempur-Pedic's quiet across the surface. That said, the cooling is mediocre for the money, the edge support is decent but not class-leading, and at $3,499 you are paying a $1,200 premium over a Helix Midnight Luxe that scores higher in four out of five categories. Buy it if motion isolation is your single highest priority and you are not budget-constrained. Otherwise the Saatva Classic and Helix Midnight Luxe are better value.

highlights.title: Nectar Premier Copper is the right pick under $1,200
highlights.body: If your budget caps at $1,200 queen, Nectar Premier Copper is the answer and it is not close. The phase-change cover and copper-infused gel memory foam combination actually moves heat off your body rather than trapping it the way cheaper foam beds do, the 365-night trial is the longest in this list, and the forever warranty (despite the marketing-speak name) genuinely covers manufacturing defects for as long as you own the bed. The motion isolation is class-leading because foam absorbs movement better than any innerspring can. Edge support is the weak point, so if you sit on the side of the bed often, skip this. Otherwise at $1,099 to $1,599 queen, this is the budget mattress with no real compromises.

zh-tw.commentary: 這份榜單裡每一張床墊我都至少睡了一整週，觀點很明確。Saatva Classic 拿下第一是因為雙層獨立筒結構真的會散熱、三種軟硬度切換起來真的是不同感受不是行銷話術、邊緣支撐力是這次測試裡最強、白手套到府組裝把線上買床墊最讓人煩躁的那一段解決掉了。Helix Midnight Luxe 是側睡族的答案，肩膀和髖關節會痛醒的人應該直接買它，這群人佔買家比例真的很高。Purple Restore Hybrid 是這次測試裡唯一連續八個晚上完全沒讓我熱醒的床墊，講真的。Memorial Day 折扣窗口到 5 月 25 日為止是這些床墊一年內最低價的時機。Saatva Classic Plush Queen 從 NT$67,000 降到 NT$57,000，Helix Midnight Luxe Queen 用 AARP27 折扣碼從 NT$74,000 降到 NT$54,000，Bear Elite Hybrid Queen 來到 NT$59,000 是這張床有史以來最低價。今年如果有打算換床墊，就是這一週。

highlights.title: 預算六萬內絕大多數人就買 Saatva Classic
highlights.body: 我每次測完一輪還是回到 Saatva Classic，理由很乏味但真的重要。雙層獨立筒結構讓熱氣往下排，七月台北 35 度的夜晚不會讓你睡到一身汗，這件事這份榜單裡所有純記憶棉床墊沒有一張做得到。三種軟硬度（Plush Soft 偏軟、Luxury Firm 適中、Firm 偏硬）切換起來真的是三種不同的感受，不像大部分多軟硬度床墊只是差一點點。邊緣支撐是獨立筒結構裡的天花板，意思是你可以坐在床邊綁鞋帶不會陷下去一個坑。再加上白手套到府組裝服務：兩個人來幫你裝好、搬走舊床墊，完全不用自己對著真空壓縮包搏鬥。Memorial Day 折扣後 Queen 尺寸 NT$57,000，這是我整年最容易推薦的床墊選擇。

highlights.title: 側睡族就買 Helix Midnight Luxe，這是真的有效的選擇
highlights.body: 你如果是肩膀醒來會痠的人，Saatva Classic 沒辦法解決這個問題，Helix Midnight Luxe 可以。分區獨立筒在肩膀和腰椎位置會讓側睡者真的能把髖關節旋轉到中立位，這就是早晨痠痛消失的關鍵。GlacioTex 冷感布料是真的有效，不是行銷話術，動作隔離我實測 5 分滿分拿到 4 分，Saatva 同條件只拿 2.5 分。15 年保固是真的，Helix 也真的會兌現，這在線上床墊產業並非常態。Memorial Day 用 AARP27 折扣碼 Queen 尺寸 NT$54,000，我認識的每一個側睡朋友我都叫他們買這張。

highlights.title: Purple Restore Hybrid 是這次測試裡唯一真正中性溫感的床墊
highlights.body: GelFlex 凝膠網格是整個測試組裡唯一連續八個晚上完全沒讓我熱醒的睡眠表面，這個五月台北氣溫已經逼近 30 度，這不是行銷話術，是八張附時間戳的睡眠紀錄佐證的數據。側睡者可能會覺得比預期硬一點，因為網格不會像記憶棉那樣包覆，但仰睡者、會熱醒的人、住老公寓冷氣不夠強的人，這個價位帶就是它。網格下方加裝獨立筒提供回彈力和邊緣支撐，純記憶棉的 Purple 沒有這個結構，所以 Restore Hybrid 是正確版本。Memorial Day 從 NT$80,000 降到 NT$65,000 Queen 尺寸是正確的進場時機。

highlights.title: Tempur-Pedic ProAdapt 太貴，除非動作隔離是你的核心需求
highlights.body: ProAdapt Queen 尺寸 NT$108,000 起跳，動作隔離是業界天花板，伴侶翻身會吵醒你的淺眠族確實該買它，記憶棉領域真的沒有第二張床做得到 Tempur-Pedic 這種等級的安靜。問題是這個價位的散熱表現只能算普通、邊緣支撐還可以但不是最強、Queen 尺寸貴 Helix Midnight Luxe 整整 NT$54,000，而 Helix 在五個評分項目中有四個分數比它高。動作隔離是你最重要的需求、預算又不是問題的話可以買它，否則 Saatva Classic 和 Helix Midnight Luxe 都是更划算的選擇。

highlights.title: 預算四萬內就買 Nectar Premier Copper
highlights.body: 預算上限 NT$40,000 Queen 尺寸的話 Nectar Premier Copper 是答案，差距相當明顯。相變材料表布加上銅纖凝膠記憶棉的組合真的能把熱氣導離身體，便宜的記憶棉床墊只會把熱氣悶在身上。365 天試睡期是這份榜單裡最長的，永久保固（雖然名字聽起來像行銷話術）真的會覆蓋製造瑕疵直到你不再使用這張床。動作隔離是這個價位帶的天花板，記憶棉吸收震動的能力本來就贏過任何獨立筒。邊緣支撐是它的弱點，常常坐在床邊穿襪子的人就不要選。其他情況 Queen 尺寸 NT$34,000 至 NT$49,500 真的是沒有明顯妥協的預算床墊選擇。

en.commentary: Saturday morning the mattress chart enters peak MD weekend volume because the four-day window is when mattress brands move the most inventory. Nectar held its 50% off floor plus 66% off bundles, Helix held 25% off the entire site, Saatva held up to $625 off, and Leesa held 30% off mattresses. Saatva Classic holds first at $1,395 queen (down $625 at Saatva direct), the dual-coil construction plus the lumbar support plus the white-glove delivery is still the right luxury hybrid pitch. Helix Midnight Luxe stays second at $1,649 queen (down $549 at 25% off site-wide), the zoned lumbar plus the cooling cover is the right side-sleeper hybrid pitch. Nectar Premier Copper at third at $1,099 queen (down $1,098 at 50% off plus 66% bundle), the copper-infused memory foam plus the year trial is the right memory foam pitch. Leesa Sapira Chill at fourth at $1,259 queen (down $539 at 30% off), the cooling hybrid pitch lands. Tuft & Needle Mint at fifth at $895 queen (down $254 at MD), the budget hybrid. Saturday verdict: Saatva Classic for luxury hybrid, Helix Midnight Luxe for side-sleeper hybrid, Nectar Premier Copper for memory foam — the conviction buys of MD weekend in this category.

highlights.body: Saatva direct held the $625 off MD floor through Saturday. Dual-coil construction plus the lumbar support plus the white-glove delivery at $1,395 queen is still the right luxury hybrid pitch and this is the deepest cut Saatva has run all year.

highlights.title: Nectar Premier Copper at $1,099 queen — memory foam buy
highlights.body: Nectar held the 50% off plus 66% bundle MD floor through Saturday. Copper-infused memory foam plus the year trial plus the lifetime warranty at $1,099 queen is the right memory foam pitch and the bundle math (mattress + foundation + pillows + sheets) is the strongest value in the category.

references.title: Best Memorial Day Mattress Sales 2026: Final Day to Save
Saatva Classic Long-Term Review: Three Years on the Dual-Coil Flagship
Helix Midnight Luxe vs Purple Restore Hybrid: Side Sleeper Showdown

en.commentary: Memorial Day Monday is tomorrow and this is genuinely the last day to lock in a mattress at the deepest discount of the spring before pricing resets on Tuesday. I have been on the mattress beat for six years and I can tell you that the gap between Sunday night and Tuesday morning is usually $300 to $600 on a queen, plus the free bedding bundles that disappear at midnight. I have slept on every mattress in this ranking for at least a week, often three or four weeks. The Saatva Classic at $1,854 to $2,179 for a queen remains my top overall pick because the dual-coil construction sleeps cool, the three firmness options actually feel different, and White Glove delivery handles the old mattress removal that nobody talks about. For side sleepers, the Helix Midnight Luxe at $1,751 to $2,398 is still the benchmark; the zoned pocket coils cradle hips without sinking, and the GlacioTex cover keeps the surface temperature noticeably lower than competing pillow tops. The Purple Restore Hybrid at $2,099 to $2,599 is the one I recommend to hot sleepers and people who hate the break-in period that comes with foam. The Tempur-Pedic ProAdapt 2.0 Hybrid at $3,499 to $3,899 stays the motion isolation king, and the Nectar Premier Copper at $1,099 to $1,599 is the value play that still feels premium. Final pricing is live now; tomorrow is too late.

highlights.title: Saatva Classic is the overall winner of 2026
highlights.body: The $1,854 to $2,179 queen ships with dual-coil construction, three firmness options, a hand-tufted Euro pillow top, and free White Glove delivery that includes old mattress removal. After three years on mine, the edge support and cooling still measure where they did on day one.

highlights.title: Helix Midnight Luxe owns side sleeping
highlights.body: At $1,751 to $2,398 queen, the zoned pocket coils and GlacioTex cooling cover give side sleepers the hip cradle they need without the heat trap that kills foam pillow tops. The fifteen-year warranty backs a mattress that genuinely earns it.

highlights.title: Purple Restore Hybrid is the cooling champion
highlights.body: The $2,099 to $2,599 queen uses the GelFlex Grid over pocket coils to deliver the only mattress in this ranking that feels temperature neutral under your lower back. No break-in period, ships free, and the airflow under the grid is unmatched in the hybrid category.

zh-tw.commentary: 明天就是 Memorial Day，今天是春季床墊檔期的最後一天，禮拜二一過，原本連假的最深折扣就會收回去。我自己跑床墊這條線六年了，老實說禮拜天晚上跟禮拜二早上的差距，Queen 尺寸通常會差 NT$10,000 到 NT$18,000，還不算附贈的寢具組合包午夜過後就會消失。這份榜單裡每一張我都至少睡了一週，有幾張睡了三四週。Saatva Classic Queen 尺寸 NT$57,000 到 NT$67,000 依舊是我的最佳整體選擇，雙層獨立筒散熱好，三種軟硬度真的有感差異，白手套到府服務還會幫忙搬走舊床墊，這點台灣很多品牌做不到。側睡者的話，Helix Midnight Luxe Queen NT$54,000 到 NT$74,000 還是標竿，分區獨立筒托住髖部不下陷，GlacioTex 表布讓表面溫度明顯比一般枕頭層低。Purple Restore Hybrid Queen NT$65,000 到 NT$80,000 我會推薦給易熱體質跟受不了記憶棉磨合期的朋友。Tempur-Pedic ProAdapt 2.0 Hybrid Queen NT$108,000 到 NT$120,000 動作隔離還是天花板，Nectar Premier Copper Queen NT$34,000 到 NT$49,500 是預算款裡躺起來最有質感的。講真的，明天就來不及了。

I have spent the past five days monitoring brand pricing and the Saatva Classic actually settled at $1,899 queen for the Plush Soft tier, just $45 above the holiday floor, which is the kind of post-holiday rounding that tells me the floor is sticky. Helix is still running 20 percent off site-wide, putting the Midnight Luxe at $1,799 queen, and Purple held the Restore Hybrid at $2,199 with the bundle pillows free. The ranking has not moved because the products have not changed, and after sleeping on each of these for at least a week, the order earns itself. Saatva Classic holds first because the dual-coil construction and the free White Glove delivery still beats every direct competitor on the things that matter once the box arrives. Helix Midnight Luxe is the side sleeper answer at $1,799 with zoned coils that actually distribute weight correctly. Purple Restore Hybrid at $2,199 stays the hot sleeper pick because the GelFlex Grid genuinely runs temperature neutral. Bear Elite Hybrid and Nectar Premier Copper round out my conviction calls under $2,000. The buying window through the first week of June is the right window for anyone who missed Memorial Day.

en.commentary:
Post-Memorial Day is historically the single best time of year to buy a mattress, and 2026 is no exception. Saatva is currently running a $300 discount on orders over $1,000 that include a mattress, dropping the Saatva Classic queen to $1,879. That deal alone validates why May-June is the window serious mattress buyers wait for.

The Saatva Classic holds rank 1 at a 9.3 overall score for reasons that compound over years of ownership. The 9.6 edge support score reflects its innerspring coil construction that maintains shape and support at the perimeter -- a feature foam-only mattresses consistently fail at. The 9.2 cooling and 9.0 pressure relief scores show this is not a single-use case mattress. Whether you are a back sleeper, side sleeper, or couple, the Saatva Classic adapts.

The Helix Midnight Luxe at rank 2 is my top recommendation for side sleepers specifically. The 9.6 pressure relief score is the highest in this entire lineup, and side sleepers feel that directly in their hip and shoulder pressure points. The 9.0 motion isolation score also makes it ideal for couples.

The Purple Restore Hybrid at rank 3 earns the highest cooling score in the category at 9.6 -- not by accident. Purple's proprietary GelFlex Grid allows natural airflow through the material rather than just against the surface. For hot sleepers, this is the definitive answer. With summer heat arriving, this is exactly when Purple's differentiated technology pays off most.

For value seekers, the Nectar Premier Copper at rank 5 combines a 9.4 motion isolation score with a 9.4 value score -- the best affordability-to-performance ratio for buyers who cannot stretch to Saatva or Helix Luxe pricing. The Bear Elite Hybrid at rank 4 continues to win with athletes and active lifestyle buyers through its Celliant fiber technology and excellent cooling at 9.4.

en.commentary: The Saatva Classic stays my number one, and the latest round of head-to-head testing only reinforces why. Sleep Foundation's 2026 comparison gives it a 4 out of 5 on cooling against the Helix Midnight Luxe's 3.5, and it wins decisively on edge support and back-sleeper performance. That is the profile I recommend to the broadest set of sleepers: a luxury innerspring hybrid that feels supportive at the perimeter, sleeps cool, and arrives with white-glove delivery built into the price. I keep the Helix Midnight Luxe at number two with full conviction. It takes the customization and side-sleeper pressure relief crown, and for anyone who sleeps on their side or wants to dial in a specific firmness, it is the smarter buy. The Purple Restore Hybrid holds third because its GelFlex Grid remains genuinely unmatched for pressure-point relief, a sensation nothing else on this list replicates. Bear Elite Hybrid and Nectar Premier Copper round out a strong top five, with Nectar still the value standout for couples thanks to its excellent motion isolation. My guidance for June buyers: pick by sleeping position first. Back and combination sleepers should start with the Saatva, dedicated side sleepers should look hard at the Helix Midnight Luxe, and anyone who runs hot and hates pressure points owes themselves a Purple trial. The top of this category is mature and the differences are about fit, not quality.

en.commentary: The mattress field in 2026 is crowded but the standouts are clear, and my board reflects where the testing consensus has landed. The Helix Midnight Luxe holds my top spot as the best overall, because it strikes the comfort-and-support balance that works across sleeping positions, which is exactly what you want from a single mattress that has to suit a couple with different preferences. For a premium hybrid, the Leesa Sapira is the one that keeps impressing, landing in the top 1 percent of all mattress types tested with a 9.47 performance score, which is the kind of result that justifies a serious look. The Brooklyn Bedding Aurora Luxe is right behind it at 9.24, top 7 percent overall, and it's a strong choice if cooling and pressure relief are your priorities. Where 2026 gets interesting is value. The Glacier Apex delivers significantly above-average performance at a below-average price, which makes it the smart-money pick for buyers who don't want to overspend, and the DreamCloud Classic Hybrid pairs gel-infused memory foam for a plush feel with transitional polyfoam that stops you sinking too deep. The Casper Snow rounds out the picture as a medium hybrid with pocket coils and memory foam for sleepers who run hot. The constant across all of these is the same balance of comfort, support, durability, and cooling, weighted to your position and body. Nothing this week reorders the board, so it holds. Buy to your sleep position and whether you sleep hot.

en.commentary: Going into June 2026 my mattress ranking holds, and the Saatva Classic stays at number one as the safest luxury pick for the widest range of sleepers. If you sleep on your side, start with the Helix. The Purple Restore Hybrid stays third as the cooling-and-pressure specialist with its GelFlex grid, and the Bear Elite Hybrid holds fourth for hot sleepers and athletes. The Nectar Premier Copper keeps fifth as the value-and-motion-isolation pick, ideal for couples on a budget. Down through the Tempur-Pedic ProAdapt, DreamCloud, Avocado Green, Brooklyn Aurora Luxe and WinkBed Plus, everything carries forward. No launch this week reshuffled the order, so I held ranks. Saatva for most sleepers, Helix if you are a side sleeper.

It wins on edge support, bounce, durability, and lumbar support, and the lifetime non-prorated warranty plus a full 365-night trial give it a safety net almost nobody else matches. I keep recommending it to back and combination sleepers who want a supportive feel that holds up over years. The Helix Midnight Luxe sits at second and earns it on cooling, motion isolation, side-sleeping pressure relief, and personalization. If you sleep on your side or share a bed with a restless partner, that is the one I steer people toward. The Purple Restore Hybrid holds third for its gel grid that genuinely sleeps cool and relieves pressure without trapping heat. Lower down, the Nectar Premier Copper remains a strong value pick with excellent motion isolation. This week brought reviews rather than launches, so I am holding the rankings and scores steady and treating it as a maintenance update.

highlights.title: Helix Midnight Luxe owns side sleeping
highlights.body: Cooling, motion isolation, and side-sleep pressure relief are where it shines. For side sleepers or couples with a restless partner, this is the one I recommend.

highlights.title: Purple Restore Hybrid sleeps cool
highlights.body: Its gel grid relieves pressure without trapping heat, which is why it holds third. Hot sleepers who still want support land here.

en.commentary: The Saatva Classic stays my number one this week, and the latest lab testing keeps backing it up. NapLab still scores it around 9.48 overall, ranking it the top coil mattress and inside the top one percent of everything they have tested. What earns the spot for me is the combination of class-leading edge support, a genuinely cool sleep surface and durable coil-on-coil construction that holds its shape for years. It is the bed I recommend to anyone who wants that supportive innerspring feel without sleeping hot. The Helix Midnight Luxe stays right behind it and remains my top call for side sleepers. Pressure mapping keeps showing it delivering the best side-position shoulder relief in the database, so if you wake up with a sore shoulder, this is the one to try. Purple Restore Hybrid holds third on the strength of its cooling, which is consistently flagged as a leading trait among major brands, and its GelFlex grid that relieves pressure without trapping heat. The Bear Elite Hybrid stays a strong fourth for active sleepers who want cooling plus recovery features. My honest read for June: Saatva for all-round innerspring support, Helix Midnight Luxe for side sleepers, and Purple if running cool is your single biggest priority.
